get.summary: Get Api V0 Meta Tasks
Get Api V1 Classes
get.description: Auto-generated documentation for GET /api/v1/classes. Supports cursor pagination through the `limit`, `sort`, and `cursor` query parameters. The exact total hit count is returned in the `X-Total-Count` response header, and the next page cursor is returned in the `X-Next-Cursor` response header.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100. Maximum is 250.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Comma-separated sort fields. Cursor pagination uses the requested sort order and appends a stable tie-breaker automatically.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the X-Next-Cursor response header from a previous page. Supply it unchanged to fetch the next page.

200.description: Classes matching optional query filters The response body contains the current page items as a JSON array. Use the `X-Total-Count` header for the exact number of matching results. Use the `X-Next-Cursor` header, when present, to request the next page.

X-Next-Cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. This header is omitted when there are no more results.

X-Total-Count.description: Exact total number of results matching the current filter set, independent of cursor pagination.

get.summary: List all permissions for a given group on a namespace

put.summary:
Replace all permissions for a group on a namespace
This removes any existing permissions and applies the new set.

post.summary:
Post a permission set to a group on a namespace
This will create a new entry if the group had no permissions,
or add to the existing entry if it did.
The body should be a JSON array of permissions:
```json
[
  "CreateObject",
  "ReadCollection"
]
```

delete.summary: Revoke a permission set from a group on a namespace

get.summary: Check a specific permission for a group on a namespace

post.summary:
Grant a specific permission to a group on a namespace
If the group previously had no permissions, a new entry is created

delete.summary: Revoke a specific permission from a group on a namespace

parameters.description: Comma-separated sort fields. Cursor pagination uses the requested sort order and appends a stable tie-breaker automatically.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the X-Next-Cursor response header from a previous page. Supply it unchanged to fetch the next page.

200.description: Object relations matching optional query filters The response body contains the current page items as a JSON array. Use the `X-Total-Count` header for the exact number of matching results. Use the `X-Next-Cursor` header, when present, to request the next page.

X-Next-Cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. This header is omitted when there are no more results.

X-Total-Count.description: Exact total number of results matching the current filter set, independent of cursor pagination.

200.description: Service is ready to receive traffic

GroupGrant.description: One group's contribution to a principal's effective permissions on a namespace.

LoginRateLimitConfigResponse.description: Effective login rate-limit configuration, echoed back in the admin state endpoint.

LoginRateLimitEntryResponse.description: One tracked rate-limit scope (a user+IP pair, an IP, or a subnet).

attempts.description: Failed attempts currently within the sliding window.

id.description: Opaque, URL-safe id; pass to `DELETE /meta/login-rate-limit/{id}` to release it.

locked.description: Whether the scope is locked out right now.

locked_for_seconds.description: Remaining lockout time in seconds, if currently locked.

entries.description: The tracked scopes matching the `include`, `scope`, and `q` query parameters.

locked_entries.description: Number of scopes currently locked out (before filtering).

returned_entries.description: Number of scopes returned in `entries` after applying the query filters.

tracked_entries.description: Total number of tracked scopes (before filtering).

LoginUser.description:
Struct to log in a user.

The password is expected to be plaintext. `name` is the principal name.

NewHubuumClassRelationFromClass.description:
To create new relations between classes from within a class
we only need the id of the class we want to relate to.

NewNamespaceWithAssignee.description:
A new namespace, with an assignee. Used for creating new namespace entries
into the database and assign all permissions to the group given as group_id.

This wraps the NewNamespace struct and uses the group_id to grant all permissions
to the group in a single transaction.

example.name: owner-report
example.template:
{% for item in items %}{{ item.name }}={{ item.data.owner }}
{% endfor %}

NewServiceAccount.description: Request body to create a service account.

scopes.description:
Optional scope set. Omit for an unscoped token; an **empty** array is
rejected (almost certainly a client bug, not "grant nothing").

NewUser.description:
Struct to create a new user.

The password is expected to be plaintext. `name` is the principal name.

PrincipalMemberResponse.description: Public representation of a group member (a principal of either kind).

PrincipalNamespacePermissions.description:
A principal's effective permissions on a single namespace, broken down by the
group that grants them.

PrincipalToken.description:
A persisted bearer token, keyed to a principal, with a full lifecycle. The
`token` field stores the HMAC hash, never the raw value.

PrincipalTokenMetadata.description: Public, hash-free projection of a token for listing.

RemoteTargetID.description: Identifier wrapper for a remote target.

output_expired.description:
True when output was produced but has since passed its retention window. Distinguishes an
expired report from one that was never generated (both have `output_available = false`).

example.name: owner-report
example.template:
{% for item in items %}{{ item.name }}={{ item.data.owner }}
{% endfor %}

ServiceAccountResponse.description:
Public response shape, combining the service-account row with its principal
name (the name lives on `principals`).

example.name: owner-report-v2
example.template:
{% for item in items %}{{ item.name }}
{% endfor %}

UpdateServiceAccount.description: Mutable fields on a service account.

UpdateUser.description:
Struct to update a user.

The password, if present, is expected to be plaintext. The name lives on the
principal; renaming is handled via the principal, not here.

UserResponse.description:
Public representation of a user, including the name resolved from the
principal (the name authority).
